bead.title: ddx work --watch: surface low claim-success-rate over a rolling window
bead.description:
PROBLEM
Silent idle on a full queue is a stuck state masquerading as healthy. During the Fizeau idle (2026-05-18), ddx work --watch kept polling (resource.preflight ok -> loop.idle every ~50s) while 23 beads sat ready and zero were claimed; the commit log was empty for the same 30-minute window. From the outside the worker looked alive. There was no top-level operator-visible signal that the claim success rate had collapsed to 0%.

ROOT CAUSE
The watch loop emits loop.idle at cli/internal/agent/execute_bead_loop.go:1513 but tracks no rolling claim-success metric, so a 0% claim rate over a full queue produces the same output as a legitimately empty queue. The --watch flag is defined at cli/cmd/work.go:88.

PROPOSED FIX (parent proposed-fix E)
Track claim attempts versus successes over a configurable rolling window (default 10 attempts). When the success rate over that window falls below a configurable threshold (e.g. 0% over 10 attempts), surface an operator-visible warning via stderr and/or a status surface (ddx bead status or a ddx work status view). Add --claim-rate-window and --claim-rate-threshold flags to ddx work.

NON-SCOPE
- Decoder coercion (sibling decoder child).
- CI schema drift gate (sibling child).
- Same-fingerprint warn escalation (sibling escalation child; complementary but distinct trigger).
- README documentation (sibling child).

CROSS-REF
Governing artifact: AR-2026-05-17 follow-up beads. Adjacent silent-failure bugs: ddx-57c40485, ddx-8f2e0ebf.
bead.acceptance:
1. ddx work --watch tracks claim success over a rolling window (default 10 attempts) and emits an operator-visible warning when the success rate falls below the configured threshold; verified by a focused test, e.g. TestWorkWatchLowClaimRateWarns in cli/internal/agent.
2. --claim-rate-window and --claim-rate-threshold flags exist on ddx work and are honored (defaults documented).
3. cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/... ./cmd/... green.
4. lefthook run pre-commit passes.
